title: Iran War Live Updates: Iran Disputes Trump’s Assertion of ‘Very Strong Talks’
summary: President Trump said he was postponing strikes on Iran’s power plants until at least Friday to allow for negotiations, but Iran cast the claim as a ploy to soothe markets and to buy time for more military action.

title: Canada’s Military Wants to Prove It Can Defend the High Arctic
summary: Canadian soldiers transported M777 howitzers to the High Arctic to show their ability to fight in an increasingly contested part of the world. It did not go as planned.

title: Sidelined by War With Iran, Gaza Residents Remain in Limbo
summary: The new war has led to panic buying and a surge in food prices for Gazans as they try to recover from Israel’s two-year offensive against Hamas.

title: A Choice of Deadly Drones Is Only a Few Clicks Away for Ukrainian Troops
summary: Ukraine has created online marketplaces to let units select their own drones, a break from generations of standardized and centralized weapons procurement.

title: Caught Between Two Conflicts, Afghans Flee Iran
summary: The war in Iran is choking off Afghanistan’s main economic lifeline and has forced at least 70,000 Afghan workers and students there back home to a nation embroiled in another conflict.

title: Taiwan Debates Military Spending as Choices Over U.S. and China Loom
summary: Tensions in the opposition Nationalist Party could surface this week as lawmakers argue over additional defense funding intended to counter Beijing’s growing might.

title: Colombian Military Plane Crash Injures Dozens

title: Canada May Need to Lean on the US as Military Threats in the Arctic Rise
summary: For the past seven decades, Canada has been the junior partner in a military agreement with the United States to protect the Canadian Arctic.

title: On Beirut’s Waterfront, Loss Meets Life, and Luxury, Amid War
summary: Thousands displaced by Israeli strikes on Lebanon’s capital now shelter along the promenade hugging the Mediterranean Sea. They share it with joggers, cyclists and dog walkers, alongside dizzying displays of wealth.

title: Attack on a Sudan Hospital Kills Dozens, Head of W.H.O. Says
summary: The group’s director general said 13 children were among those killed in the latest violence in the Darfur region, and he decried the targeting of health care facilities in the civil war.

summary: Fatih Birol, the leader of the International Energy Agency, said the Iran war was a bigger crisis than the two oil shocks in the 1970s combined.
